heading: Foundation for Food and Agriculture Research
label: 5939 Foundation for Food and Agriculture Research
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Agricultural Act of 2014, and not as part of subtitle H of title XVI of the Food, Agriculture, Conservation, and Trade Act of 1990 which comprises this subchapter.

children.heading: Board
children.label: 1 Board
children.text: The term “Board” means the Board of Directors described in subsection (e).

children.heading: Department
children.label: 2 Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Agriculture.

children.heading: Foundation
children.label: 3 Foundation
children.text: The term “Foundation” means the Foundation for Food and Agriculture Research established under subsection (b).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 4 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a nonprofit corporation to be known as the “Foundation for Food and Agriculture Research”.

children.heading: Status
children.label: 2 Status
children.text: The Foundation shall not be an agency or instrumentality of the United States Government.

children.label: A
children.text: plant health, production, and plant products;

children.label: B
children.text: animal health, production, and products;

children.label: C
children.text: food safety, nutrition, and health;

children.label: D
children.text: renewable energy, natural resources, and the environment;

children.label: E
children.text: agricultural and food security;

children.label: F
children.text: agriculture systems and technology; and

children.label: G
children.text: agriculture economics and rural communities; and

to foster collaboration with agricultural researchers from the Federal Government, State (as defined in ) governments, institutions of higher education (as defined in ), industry, and nonprofit organizations.section 3103 of this titlesection 1001 of title 20

children.label: A
children.text: award grants to, or enter into contracts, memoranda of understanding, or cooperative agreements with, scientists and entities, which may include agricultural research agencies in the Department, university consortia, public-private partnerships, institutions of higher education, nonprofit organizations, and industry, to efficiently and effectively advance the goals and priorities of the Foundation;

children.label: i
children.text: identify existing and proposed Federal intramural and extramural research and development programs relating to the purposes of the Foundation described in subsection (c); and

children.label: ii
children.text: coordinate Foundation activities with those programs so as to minimize duplication of existing efforts and to avoid conflicts, specifically at the Department of Agriculture; and

children.label: iii
children.text: document the consultation process and include a summary of the results in the annual report required in subsection (f)(3)(B)1So in original. Probably should be followed by a period.1

children.label: C
children.text: identify unmet and emerging agricultural research needs after reviewing the roadmap for agricultural research, education, and extension authorized by ;section 7614a of this title

children.label: D
children.text: facilitate technology transfer and release of information and data gathered from the activities of the Foundation to the agricultural research community and agriculture stakeholders;

children.label: E
children.text: promote and encourage the development of the next generation of agricultural research scientists; and

children.label: F
children.text: carry out such other activities as the Board determines to be consistent with the purposes of the Foundation.

children.heading: Relationship to other activities
children.label: 2 Relationship to other activities
children.text: The activities described in paragraph (1) shall be supplemental to any other activities at the Department and shall not preempt any authority or responsibility of the Department under another provision of law.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Foundation shall be governed by a Board of Directors.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Board shall be composed of appointed and ex-officio, nonvoting members.

children.label: i
children.text: The Secretary.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Under Secretary of Agriculture for Research, Education, and Economics.

children.label: iii
children.text: The Administrator of the Agricultural Research Service.

children.label: iv
children.text: The Director of the National Institute of Food and Agriculture.

children.label: v
children.text: The Director of the National Science Foundation.

children.label: I
children.text: 8 shall be selected from a list of candidates to be provided by the National Academy of Sciences; and

children.label: II
children.text: 7 shall be selected from lists of candidates provided by industry.

children.heading: Expertise
children.label: I Expertise
children.text: The ex-officio members shall ensure that a majority of the appointed members of the Board have actual experience in agriculture or agricultural research and, to the extent practicable, represent diverse sectors of agriculture.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: II Limitation
children.text: No employee of the Federal Government may serve as an appointed member of the Board under this subparagraph.

children.heading: Not Federal employment
children.label: III Not Federal employment
children.text: Appointment to the Board under this subparagraph shall not constitute Federal employment.

children.heading: Authority
children.label: iii Authority
children.text: All appointed members of the Board shall be voting members.

children.heading: Chair
children.label: D Chair
children.text: The Board shall, from among the members of the Board, designate an individual to serve as Chair of the Board.

children.label: A
children.text: to incorporate the Foundation; and

children.label: B
children.text: to appoint the members of the Board in accordance with paragraph (2)(C)(i).

children.label: I
children.text: policies for the selection of future Board members, officers, employees, agents, and contractors of the Foundation;

children.label: aa
children.text: the acceptance, solicitation, and disposition of donations and grants to the Foundation; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the disposition of assets of the Foundation, including appropriate limits on the ability of donors to designate, by stipulation or restriction, the use or recipient of donated funds;

children.label: III
children.text: policies that would subject all employees, fellows, trainees, and other agents of the Foundation (including members of the Board) to conflict of interest standards in the same manner as Federal employees are subject to the conflict of interest standards under ;section 208 of title 18

children.label: IV
children.text: policies for writing, editing, printing, publishing, and vending of books and other materials;

children.label: V
children.text: policies for the conduct of the general operations of the Foundation, including a cap on administrative expenses for recipients of a grant, contract, or cooperative agreement from the Foundation; and

children.label: VI
children.text: specific duties for the Executive Director;

children.label: ii
children.text: prioritize and provide overall direction for the activities of the Foundation;

children.label: iii
children.text: evaluate the performance of the Executive Director;

children.label: iv
children.text: actively solicit and accept funds, gifts, grants, devises, or bequests of real or personal property made to the Foundation, including from private entities; and

children.label: v
children.text: carry out any other necessary activities regarding the Foundation.

children.label: i
children.text: reflect unfavorably on the ability of the Foundation to carry out the duties of the Foundation in a fair and objective manner; or

children.label: ii
children.text: compromise, or appear to compromise, the integrity of any governmental agency or program, or any officer or employee employed by, or involved in, a governmental agency or program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term of each member of the Board appointed under paragraph (2)(C) shall be 5 years, except that of the members initially appointed, 8 of the members shall each be appointed for a term of 3 years and 7 of the members shall each be appointed for a term of 2 years.

children.heading: Partial terms
children.label: ii Partial terms
children.text: If a member of the Board does not serve the full term applicable under clause (i), the individual appointed to fill the resulting vacancy shall be appointed for the remainder of the term of the predecessor of the individual.

children.heading: Transition
children.label: iii Transition
children.text: A member of the Board may continue to serve after the expiration of the term of the member until a successor is appointed.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: B Vacancies
children.text: After the initial appointment of the members of the Board under paragraph (2)(C), any vacancy in the membership of the Board shall be filled as provided in the bylaws established under paragraph (4)(A)(i).

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: 6 Compensation
children.text: Members of the Board may not receive compensation for service on the Board but may be reimbursed for travel, subsistence, and other necessary expenses incurred in carrying out the duties of the Board.

children.heading: Meetings and quorum
children.label: 7 Meetings and quorum
children.text: A majority of the members of the Board shall constitute a quorum for purposes of conducting the business of the Board.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Board shall hire an Executive Director who shall carry out such duties and responsibilities as the Board may prescribe.

children.heading: Service
children.label: B Service
children.text: The Executive Director shall serve at the pleasure of the Board.

children.label: i
children.text: adopt, alter, and use a corporate seal, which shall be judicially noticed;

children.label: ii
children.text: hire, promote, compensate, and discharge 1 or more officers, employees, and agents, as may be necessary, and define the duties of the officers, employees, and agents;

children.label: iii
children.text: solicit and accept funds, gifts, grants, devises, or bequests of real or personal property made to the Foundation, including such support from private entities;

children.label: I
children.text: real or personal property of the Foundation is acquired, held, and transferred;

children.label: II
children.text: general operations of the Foundation are to be conducted; and

children.label: III
children.text: the privileges granted to the Board by law are exercised and enjoyed;

children.label: v
children.text: with the consent of the applicable executive department or independent agency, use the information, services, and facilities of the department or agency in carrying out this section on a reimbursable basis;

children.label: vi
children.text: enter into contracts with public and private organizations for the writing, editing, printing, and publishing of books and other material;

children.label: vii
children.text: hold, administer, invest, and spend any funds, gifts, grant, devise, or bequest of real or personal property made to the Foundation;

children.label: viii
children.text: enter into such contracts, leases, cooperative agreements, and other transactions as the Board considers appropriate to conduct the activities of the Foundation;

children.label: ix
children.text: modify or consent to the modification of any contract or agreement to which the Foundation is a party or in which the Foundation has an interest;

children.label: x
children.text: take such action as may be necessary to obtain and maintain patents for and to license inventions (as defined in ) developed by the Foundation, employees of the Foundation, or derived from the collaborative efforts of the Foundation;section 201 of title 35

children.label: xi
children.text: sue and be sued in the corporate name of the Foundation, and complain and defend in courts of competent jurisdiction;

children.label: xii
children.text: appoint other groups of advisors as may be determined necessary to carry out the functions of the Foundation; and

children.label: xiii
children.text: exercise such other incidental powers as are necessary to carry out the duties and functions of the Foundation in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: No appointed member of the Board or officer or employee of the Foundation or of any program established by the Foundation (other than ex-officio members of the Board) shall exercise administrative control over any Federal employee.

children.label: i
children.text: provide for annual audits of the financial condition of the Foundation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: make the audits, and all other records, documents, and other papers of the Foundation, available to the Secretary and the Comptroller General of the United States for examination or audit.

children.label: aa
children.text: a description of Foundation activities, including accomplishments and how those activities align to the challenges identified in the strategic plan under clause (iv);

children.label: bb
children.text: a comprehensive statement of the operations and financial condition of the Foundation; and

children.label: cc
children.text: a description of available agricultural research programs and priorities for the upcoming fiscal year.

children.label: aa
children.text: the source of the gifts, grants, devises, or bequests; and

children.label: bb
children.text: any restrictions on the purposes for which the gift, grant, devise, or bequest may be used.

children.label: aa
children.text: make copies of each report submitted under subclause (I) available for public inspection; and

children.label: bb
children.text: on request, provide a copy of the report to any individual.

children.heading: Public meeting
children.label: IV Public meeting
children.text: The Board shall hold an annual public meeting to summarize the activities of the Foundation.

children.heading: Grant reporting
children.label: ii Grant reporting
children.text: Any recipient of a grant under subsection (d)(1)(A) shall provide the Foundation with a report at the conclusion of any research or studies conducted that describes the results of the research or studies, including any data generated.

children.label: I
children.text: a schedule for funding competitions;

children.label: II
children.text: a discussion of how applications for funding will be evaluated; and

children.label: III
children.text: how the Foundation will communicate information about funded awards to the public to ensure that grantees and partners understand the objectives of the Foundation.

children.label: I
children.text: a forecast of major agricultural challenge opportunities identified by the scientific advisory councils of the Foundation and approved by the Board, including short- and long-term objectives;

children.label: aa
children.text: processes relating to grant awards, including the selection, review, and notification processes;

children.label: bb
children.text: communication of past, current, and future research priorities; and

children.label: cc
children.text: plans to solicit and respond to public input on the opportunities identified in the strategic plan;

children.label: aa
children.text: raising funds in amounts greater than the amounts required under subsection (g)(1)(B);

children.label: bb
children.text: soliciting additional resources pursuant to subsections (e)(4)(A)(iv) and (f)(2)(A)(iii); and

children.label: cc
children.text: managing and leveraging such resources pursuant to subsection (f)(2)(A)(vii); and

children.label: IV
children.text: other related issues, as determined by the Board.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: To ensure integrity in the operations of the Foundation, the Board shall develop and enforce procedures relating to standards of conduct, financial disclosure statements, conflicts of interest (including recusal and waiver rules), audits, and any other matters determined appropriate by the Board.

children.label: i
children.text: the individual;

children.label: ii
children.text: a relative (as defined in ) of that individual; orsection 13101 of title 5

children.label: iii
children.text: a business organization or other entity in which the individual has an interest, including an organization or other entity with which the individual is negotiating employment.

children.heading: Intellectual property
children.label: 5 Intellectual property
children.text: The Board shall adopt written standards to govern the ownership and licensing of any intellectual property rights derived from the collaborative efforts of the Foundation.

children.heading: Liability
children.label: 6 Liability
children.text: The United States shall not be liable for any debts, defaults, acts, or omissions of the Foundation nor shall the full faith and credit of the United States extend to any obligations of the Foundation.

children.heading: Establishment funding
children.label: i Establishment funding
children.text: On , of the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation, the Secretary shall transfer to the Foundation to carry out this section $200,000,000, to remain available until expended under the conditions described in subparagraph (B).February 7, 2014

children.heading: Enhanced funding
children.label: ii Enhanced funding
children.text: On the date on which the strategic plan described in subsection (f)(3)(B)(iv) is submitted, of the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation, the Secretary shall transfer to the Foundation to carry out this section $185,000,000, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: Additional funding
children.label: iii Additional funding
children.text: Not later than 30 days after , of the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation, the Secretary shall transfer to the Foundation to carry out this section $37,000,000, to remain available until expended.November 17, 2023

children.heading: Further funding
children.label: iv Further funding
children.text: Not later than 30 days after , of the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation, the Secretary shall transfer to the Foundation to carry out this section $37,000,000, to remain available until expended.July 4, 2025

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Foundation may use the funds made available under subparagraph (A) to carry out the purposes, duties, and powers of the Foundation only to the extent that the Foundation secures an equal amount of matching funds from a non-Federal source, including an agricultural commodity promotion, research, and information program.

children.heading: Effect
children.label: ii Effect
children.text: Nothing in this section requires the Foundation to require a matching contribution from an individual grantee as a condition of receiving a grant under this section.

children.heading: Prohibition on construction
children.label: C Prohibition on construction
children.text: None of the funds made available under subparagraph (A) may be used for construction.

children.heading: Separation of funds
children.label: 2 Separation of funds
children.text: The Executive Director shall ensure that any funds received under paragraph (1) are held in separate accounts from funds received from nongovernmental entities as described in subsection (f)(2)(A)(iii).
